title: Stable Diffusion vs MidJourney: The Prompt Testing Edition
description:
👋 Hello there and welcome back to my channel! Today, we're putting two big names in AI image generation head-to-head: Stable Diffusion and Midjourney. 🤖🎨

🛠 Tools & Models Used:

Stable Diffusion: Automatic1111 Interface, Juggernaut XL version 5
Midjourney: Model version 5.2

📝 What to Expect:
1️⃣ Testing the same prompts on both tools
2️⃣ No cherry-picking—first result is what you get!
3️⃣ My Conclusion

👇 Test Parameter
Single Prompt, no negative prompt or other tools.
Batch Size: 4 images

💡 Note:
I won't be using any extras like ControlNet or image-to-image features. We're sticking to basic text-to-image prompts.

🤔 Final Thoughts:
Stay till the end for my take on which tool performs better for different types of prompts!
👍 If this video helps you out, make sure to hit that Like button and don't forget to Subscribe for more tech insights and tutorials. Drop a comment below and let me know which tool you think did better, or if you have any other questions!

Chapters:

00:00 Introdu
transcript: Hi, everyone. In this video, I'm going to compare two powerful AI tools that turn  text into images, stable diffusion and mid-journey. I'll try out different  prompts on both of these tools to see what sets them apart. I'll be using the  Automatic 1111 interface for stable diffusion and an SDXL model called  Juggernaut XL Version 5, which you can find on the Civet AI website. I'm only  going to test text-to-image prompts to keep things simple. I'll use only  positive prompts and won't add any extensions like control net or use image  to image, because we all know that with those tools you can do pretty much  anything you want. For the technical details, I'm using the DPM plus plus plus  two M-carous sampling method with 25 sampling steps. The image dimensions will  be 1024px by 1024px and I'll generate four images in each batch, just like  mid-journey does. The CFG scale is set at 7. I won't be cherry-picking results. The  first image that comes out for each prompt is the one you will see in the  video for both stable diffusion and mid-journey. Here are my settings for  mid-journey. I'm using model version 5.2. Now I'm going to test various prompts  so you can see the differences between the two tools. Feel free to pause the  video if you want a closer look at the prompts. On the left side of the screen,  you will see images generated by stable diffusion and on the right you will see  the ones from mid-journey. The prompt used will be displayed at the bottom of the  screen.  We are a lot of way gone through the night.  My body aches, my eyes are wide. As this mistake, as this divide, it separates us.  Feel like a stranger in my skin. Before we change, we'll swear again. Like it's a game like it's  METE EVERYBODY WARS! I hope you had a good time watching the prompt  testing. Now it's time to wrap up and share my final thoughts on stable diffusion and  mid-journey AI. Keep in mind, this is just what I think. I'd love to hear your thoughts  too, so don't forget to leave a comment below. Price! If you go with stable diffusion  A1111, it's free and you can install it locally on your PC. On the other hand,  mid-journey requires a subscription and runs on Discord.  Privacy.  With stable diffusion, your data remains private.  For mid-journey, it's public by default,  but you can go private if you're willing to pay  for $60 subscription.  Speed.  Stable diffusion runs faster on an RTX 4090,  taking just 18 seconds for a set of four images.  However, it's slower on other GPUs.  Mid-journey speed depends on server traffic,  usually taking between 30 to 60 seconds.  Size for one to one ratio image.  The image size you can work with on stable diffusion  depends on your GPU and can range from 1024 to 2048 pixels.  You also have the option to upscale it.  Mid-journey, however, is limited to 1000 to 24 pixels.  The look.  Stable diffusion generally offers a more natural look  while mid-journey tends to be more artistic.  Default mood.  If you prefer a lighter and more natural mood,  stable diffusion might be your choice.  Mid-journey leans towards a darker look.  Sensation.  Stable diffusion doesn't censor your images  while mid-journey does.  Variations.  Stable diffusion provides similar results  across a set of four images  and contrast mid-journey usually gives you more variations  within those four generated images.  Text slash typography.  Both platforms aren't great in this department,  but stable diffusion gives you the option  to improve it using control net.  Image to image options.  If you want more control and advanced options,  go with stable diffusion.  Mid-journey offers limited control in this regard.  Number of models.  Stable diffusion offers a wide variety of models  through the Civite eye sight  while mid-journey has limited options  usually with the latest model being the best.  Customization.  With stable diffusion, you can train your own lower model  or use extensions for customization.  Mid-journey doesn't offer good options for this.  Ease of use.  Stable diffusion is not as easy to use as mid-journey  but gives you more control over the process.  Mid-journey is easier for beginners  but offers less control.  I personally prefer stable diffusion  while my wife, who's new to AI, likes mid-journey better.  So it really comes down to what works best for you.  That's all for today.  Have a great day.
